data.title: Is It Time To Reassess Zalando (XTRA:ZAL) After Recent Share Price Weakness?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Zalando shares at €22.02 are offering value or a value trap, you are not alone in asking that question right now.
  • The stock has moved 8% over the last week, compared with a 1.7% decline over the past month, and is still showing a 10.7% decline year to date and a 28.1% decline over the past year.
  • These price moves sit alongside ongoing interest in how Zalando is reshaping its role as a European online fashion platform and responding to changing consumer behavior. Investors are weighing how its recent updates on operations, partnerships and cost discipline fit with a longer history of weaker 3-year and 5-year returns of 36.2% and 74.6% declines respectively.
  • On our framework the company scores 2 out of 6 on undervaluation checks, giving it a valuation score of 2. Next we will look at how different methods such as multiples and discounted cash flow each frame that result, before finishing with a way to pull all those signals together more clearly.

Zalando scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Zalando Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a business might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today using a required return. It focuses on the cash you could theoretically receive as a shareholder, not just reported profits.

For Zalando, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about €340.6m. Analysts provide explicit forecasts up to 2028, with free cash flow projected at €779.9m that year. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates out to 2035, with ten year projections ranging from about €514.8m in 2026 to about €1,292.3m in 2035. All projected cash flows are discounted back to today to reflect time value and risk.

On this basis, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of €79.62 per share. Compared with the current share price of €22.02, this implies a 72.3% discount, which suggests the shares screen as significantly undervalued on this method alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Zalando is undervalued by 72.3%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 224 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Zalando.

Approach 2: Zalando Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Zalando, the P/E ratio is a handy shorthand for how much investors are paying for each euro of earnings. It ties the share price directly to the bottom line, which is usually what ultimately matters to shareholders.

What counts as a "normal" P/E depends a lot on what investors expect for future growth and how much risk they see in the business. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk often points to a lower one.

Zalando currently trades on a P/E of 23.27x. That sits close to both its peer average of 23.05x and above the Specialty Retail industry average of 15.19x. Simply Wall St also calculates a Fair Ratio of 17.28x, which is the P/E it would expect for Zalando after factoring in elements like earnings growth, industry, profit margin, market cap and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for those business specific factors rather than assuming all retailers deserve similar multiples. When set against the current 23.27x P/E, the 17.28x Fair Ratio indicates that Zalando is trading at a richer level than this framework would suggest.

Result: OVERVALUED

data.title: Natera Launches Zenith Genomics To Expand Rare Disease Testing Story
data.description:
• Natera (NasdaqGS:NTRA) has launched Zenith Genomics, a next generation whole genome sequencing assay for rare disease detection.
  • The assay is being introduced in partnership with MyOme, with exclusive collaboration around genome wide rare disease diagnostics.
  • Zenith Genomics integrates advanced methylation analysis with DNA sequencing to address difficult diagnostic questions in rare conditions.
  • The launch and supporting data are being presented at a leading medical genetics conference, targeting clinicians focused on complex hereditary disease.

Natera is already known for its genetic testing portfolio across oncology, women’s health and organ health. Zenith Genomics adds a whole genome rare disease offering to that mix. The assay uses both DNA and epigenetic information, which is designed to help clinicians address cases where standard sequencing has not provided clear answers. For investors, it represents another technology pillar tied to specialist clinical use.

The MyOme partnership and planned electronic medical record integration point to a model that aims to fit into everyday clinical workflows rather than remaining confined to research settings. As Natera references rapid expansion of actionable disease targets, you may want to watch how quickly the test menu develops and how adoption progresses among geneticists and rare disease centers.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Natera by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Natera.

3 things going right for Natera that this headline doesn't cover.

This launch is really about Natera trying to broaden its role in high complexity genomics by pairing MyOme’s multi omic platform with its own commercial reach. Zenith genomics sits alongside Signatera and the women’s and organ health tests, giving the company another use case where deep sequencing and interpretation matter. The focus on long read sequencing, methylation signatures and whole genome coverage speaks directly to rare disease cases where standard panels have not delivered clear diagnoses, which is a pain point for clinicians and health systems facing high rare disease costs.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Natera Narrative

  • Zenith supports the existing narrative that new product launches and AI oriented workflows can expand Natera’s footprint in personalized medicine and help justify its investment in genomics infrastructure.
  • The collaboration with MyOme could also stretch resources, as Natera is already funding trials and commercialization in oncology and organ health, so investors may want to watch how operating spend tracks against new revenue sources.
  • The rare disease whole genome focus and integrated methylation readout are not front and center in many oncology heavy narratives, so the potential role of this business line may be underappreciated in some storylines.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Natera to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Integrating a premium genome test into routine care depends on payer policies for comprehensive sequencing, so any pushback on reimbursement could limit volume despite clinical interest.
  • ⚠️ Competition from other genomics players such as Invitae and GeneDx, and from broader whole genome platforms at companies like Illumina’s partners, may keep pricing and margins under pressure in rare disease testing.
  • 🎁 If Zenith shortens diagnostic timelines for complex conditions, it could strengthen Natera’s relationships with geneticists and tertiary centers, supporting cross selling of its broader test menu.
  • 🎁 Automatic methylation analysis on eligible exome and genome orders from early Q2 2026 may increase test utility for clinicians without adding workflow friction, which can be a practical advantage when health systems standardize vendors.

What To Watch Going Forward

You may want to track a few things from here. First, how quickly Zenith is adopted across Natera’s existing electronic medical record integrations and whether ordering becomes routine at major rare disease centers. Second, watch for any updates on payer coverage criteria for whole genome and methylation supported testing, since those policies will influence test economics. Finally, compare Natera’s rare disease progress with updates from peers like Invitae and GeneDx to gauge how competitive its offering appears in terms of clinical utility and workflow.

Approach 1: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those amounts back to today.

For Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is NT$898,869.69m. Analyst inputs and extrapolated estimates point to projected free cash flow of NT$5,035,855.65m in 2035, with intermediate projections such as NT$1,410,285.24m in 2026 and NT$2,858,568.79m in 2029.

Discounting these future NT$ cash flows back to today results in an estimated intrinsic value of US$268.59 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$336.71, the DCF output suggests the stock is about 25.4% above this modelled value, so on this measure it screens as overvalued rather than cheap.

For a profitable business like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. This is often where the market focus sits first.

What counts as a reasonable P/E usually reflects how the market views a company’s growth potential and risk profile. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while slower expected growth or higher uncertainty tends to anchor it lower.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing currently trades on a P/E of 28.46x. That compares with an average P/E of 41.32x for the Semiconductor industry and a peer group average of 53.65x. data.title: Swire Pacific (SEHK:19) One Off HK$6.7b Loss Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative
data.description:
Swire Pacific (SEHK:19) has wrapped up FY 2025 with second half revenue of HK$44.7b, net income of HK$2.1b and basic EPS of HK$1.57, while the trailing twelve months show revenue of HK$90.5b, net income of HK$2.9b and EPS of HK$2.17. The group has seen revenue move from HK$42.4b in the second half of 2024 to HK$44.7b in the latest half, with basic EPS shifting from HK$0.29 to HK$1.57 over the same periods. This sets up a results season where investors will focus squarely on how sustainable these earnings and the compressed margins really are after the large one off loss and current 3.2% net margin profile.

See our full analysis for Swire Pacific.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to line these results up against the widely held narratives around Swire Pacific and see which stories the latest margins and earnings support and which they push back on.

HK$2.1b half year profit against HK$6.7b one off hit

  • Net income in the second half came in at HK$2.1b, while trailing 12 month net income is HK$2.9b. This sits alongside a HK$6.7b one off loss that pushed the trailing net margin to 3.2% from 5.3% a year earlier.
  • Bears focus on the risk that heavy investment in property, beverages and aviation could struggle to earn through these kinds of charges. However, the data shows recurring underlying profit of HK$9.8b and aviation recurring profit up 19%, which contrasts with the compressed reported margin that includes the HK$6.7b loss.
    • Critics highlight the exposure to Hong Kong and Mainland China property, Cathay and HAECO, and new beverage capacity in markets facing competition. Those concerns sit alongside management’s commentary that Cathay has now delivered three consecutive years of profit.
    • The bearish narrative also points to large committed capex in Chinese Mainland property and Swire Coca Cola at a time when the trailing margin is 3.2%. The key question for that view is whether recurring profit closer to HK$9.8b or the reported HK$2.9b net income better reflects future earning power.

  • Analysts are forecasting earnings to grow about 22.45% per year, compared with forecast revenue growth of 4.2% per year, which is slower than the wider Hong Kong market forecast of 8.3% a year.
  • Supporters of the bullish narrative argue that expansion of premium property brands and beverage operations across Greater China and Southeast Asia can push margins higher. That kind of story lines up with the current gap between modest revenue growth and faster expected earnings growth.
    • The consensus narrative talks about property and beverage growth in rapidly urbanising markets and ongoing capital recycling, while the trailing numbers show HK$90.5b of revenue and 3.2% net margin. Any margin lift from these projects would therefore have a clear base to be compared against.
    • Bulls also point to sustainability focused projects and a relatively low gearing ratio of 20.6% with HK$64b of liquidity and HK$23b of bank balances. They see this as giving room to fund that earnings growth even if headline revenue growth stays close to 4.2% a year.
